proposed_base_material_variables.pitting_resistance_equivalent_number: A calculated compositional index (e.g., Cr + 3xMo mass%) used to theoretically quantify and compare the relative resistance of the base alloy to localized pitting and crevice corrosion.

proposed_conditioned_material_variables.specimen_dimensions: The overall physical geometric dimensions (e.g., length, width, and thickness) of the prepared material coupons prior to environmental exposure or specific surface masking.

proposed_experiment_variables.pitting_current_density_threshold: The specific predetermined current density value (e.g., 10 µA/cm²) and accompanying behavior criteria (e.g., increasing without oscillation) used to officially define the onset of stable pitting during an anodic polarization sweep.
proposed_experiment_variables.electrolyte_pH: The exact measured pH level or hydrogen ion concentration of the testing electrolyte, including details of any acids (e.g., HCl) or bases added to adjust the acidity of the solution.
